mnemonics.title: Daemon Creation Steps
mnemonics.content: D-A-E-M-O-N: Detach from terminal, Adopted by init, Exit parent, Move to root (/), Open /dev/null, New session (setsid)

mnemonics.title: Daemon Characteristics
mnemonics.content: B-A-C-K-S: Background, Always running, Controlled by init, Keeps no working directory, Stateless background

acronyms.expansion: Detached And Executing Mysteriously Or Not
acronyms.usage: Mnemonic for understanding daemon nature - runs detached from user control
Internet super-server that spawns other daemons on network request
First process (PID 1) that starts at boot and adopts orphaned processes

visualTips.visualization: Picture a fork icon → another fork icon → then the daemon running alone. First fork creates child, parent exits; second fork prevents session leadership and terminal control.
Draw init at top, daemons as branches hanging below (not connected to any terminal). Show orphan adoption with arrows to init.
Show three pipes (stdin, stdout, stderr) all pointing to /dev/null (like a trash can), representing the null device.

analogies.analogy: Like a night janitor who works when everyone leaves, maintaining the building (system) without disturbing the workers (users).
Interactive process is like a receptionist (answers phones, helps users), while a daemon is like the HVAC system running in the basement - essential but invisible.
Like a host who only brings out chefs when guests arrive, instead of keeping all chefs cooking continuously - saves resources.
